name: Skane County (Sweden)

colors.symbolism: the gules field of the Skane arms
the gold griffin head and crown

description: A square red flag charged centrally with the gold crowned griffin's head, the historic emblem of Skane.
history: The flag is the heraldic banner of the arms of Skane County, derived from the coat of arms of the historic province of Skane (Scania); the griffin's head has been used as the provincial symbol since the 17th century. Skane County was formed in 1997 by the merger of Kristianstad and Malmohus counties.

features.symbolism: the gules field of the historic Skane provincial arms
a gold griffin's head, crowned, on a red field; the historic emblem of the province of Skane.
